section.title: Mirroring &productname; repositories with &rmt;
section.para: Use this procedure to mirror the &productname; repositories for &rhel; &productnumber;.

listitem.para: The &rmt; server is installed and up to date.

The &rmt; server has enough storage available for repository mirroring. The amount of storage required depends on the number of repositories you mirror. We recommend at least 1.5 times the total size of all enabled repositories. Be aware that the &productname; repositories will grow substantially over time.
Your organization credentials appear correctly in /etc/rmt.conf.
You have a &productname; subscription activated in the &scc;.

step.para: On the &rmt; server, update the available product and repository metadata:
Synchronization is also performed automatically by rmt-server-sync.timer. You can check the status and the next scheduled synchronization time with the following command:
Enable the &productname; &productnumber; product. This also enables all the default repositories:

listitem.para: To enable the standard product, use the product ID 2538:
To enable &ems; (EMS), find the version you need and enable it using the listed ID:

important.title: EMS disk space requirement
important.para: The standard product and the EMS product use separate repositories. You can enable both, but be aware that doing so uses significantly more disk space on the &rmt; server because it must mirror duplicate packages.
If you previously mirrored the standard repositories but now only need EMS repositories, you can clean up the standard repositories with the following commands:

step.para: If your subscription includes the &ha; extension, enable it using the product ID 2539:
If you need the Source or Debug repositories, find and enable them with the following commands:

Depending on your subscription, this command shows the standard repositories, the EMS repositories and the HA repositories. Enabled repositories have the status Mirror. Unenabled repositories have the status Don't Mirror.
Enable the repositories you need with their numeric IDs. You can enable multiple repositories at once:
Start package mirroring for the enabled repositories:
Packages are downloaded to the &rmt; server once per day by rmt-server-mirror.timer. You can check the status and the next scheduled download time with the following command:
